titleStmt.title: SII 11.20: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 20. (B.K. No. 73 of 1926-27). SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE VĪRABHADRA TEMPLE.

summary.p: The inscription which is dated in Śaka 805, Śōbhakṛit (= A.D. 883) refers itself to the reign of Akālavarsha Kannara-Bhaṭāra (i.e., Kṛishṇa II) and mentions Indapayya as governing the nāḍu, evidently the Purigeṟe-nāḍu, in which Saraṭavura was situated. It registers gift of a gōsāsa by Māsigara-Chidaṇṇa in the presence of the assembly of the Fifty (Mahājanas) of Saraṭavura.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.note: The records have since been published by Mr. N.Lakshminarayan Rao,Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XXI, pp. 206 ff.
div.p:
© svasti śrī Akālavariṣā pṛthuvī vāllava mārajādi rāja
paramēśvarā śrīmatkannara bhaṭarara rajyābhīvriddhi saluttumi
re śaka nṛpakālātīta saṁbātsaraṅga ḷeṇṭunūṟāydāne sō
bhakṛt Eṁbha saṁbhatsaraṁ pravarttise Indāpāyyā nāḍā nāḷutti
re purigeṟenāḍasaraṭavura dāyvadiṁbaru miṟdu nāḍayise mā
sigara cidāṇna gōsā
sa(ṁ)mi ḻdōṁ
stanābhivṛddhi
nama ©
Digital edition of SII 11.20 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
